user_query:
We’re planning to extend our internal AI developer assistant with vetted, high‑leverage add‑ons across our stack. Please read the following to tailor your recommendations:
- input/project_profile.json (tech stack, constraints, timeline)
- input/security_requirements.md (security and licensing guardrails)

Deliverables (write all outputs under output/):
1) output/skills_catalog.json — A curated shortlist of 8–12 AI assistant extensions relevant to our stack. For each item include exactly these fields:
   - name (string)
   - owner_repo (string in "owner/repo" format; do not include URLs)
   - category (one of: "frontend", "devops", "security", "database", "payments", "misc")
   - source_type ("official_partner" or "community")
   - use_cases (array of at least 2 short strings)
   - risk_level (one of: "low", "medium", "high")
   - risk_notes (string, at least 30 characters, tailored to our constraints)
   - rationale (string, at least 50 characters, explaining why it fits our stack and goals)
   - priority_score (number 0–100, higher is better)

   Requirements:
   - Include at least one item from a vendor we already rely on (e.g., Vercel, Stripe, Supabase, or similar) and at least one item from a community-maintained collection.
   - Cover at least three distinct categories, and ensure both "devops" and "security" are represented.
   - Use only owner/repo identifiers (no command lines or URLs).

2) output/installation_plan.json — A four‑phase adoption plan with these keys: "discovery", "poc", "canary", "production". Each key maps to an object with:
   - skills (array of owner_repo strings drawn from output/skills_catalog.json)
   - goals (string)
   - exit_criteria (string)

   Requirements:
   - Every owner_repo listed in output/skills_catalog.json must appear in at least one phase.
   - The phases must all be present and non-empty.

3) output/governance.md — 1–2 pages that define how we evaluate and approve extensions before rollout. Include clear sections for:
   - Risk & approvals workflow (reference our security and licensing guardrails)
   - Security review checklist (static analysis, supply chain checks, license review)
   - Rollback plan (how to revert if issues arise)
   - Monitoring & metrics (how we track impact and regressions)

4) output/README.md — A concise summary of the top 5 recommendations with one‑line justifications each. Include each top pick as a bullet that contains its owner_repo.

Important:
- Do not include shell or install commands; do not paste URLs. Use owner/repo references only.
- Ensure all files are syntactically valid (JSON must parse).
